required_points: The gradient provides the local linear term used by the first-order Taylor approximation.
forbidden_or_unsupported_points: The approximation is exact for every differentiable function.
Using the gradient guarantees a small approximation error.

required_points: Gradient Descent uses the gradient to choose its descent direction.
forbidden_or_unsupported_points: Gradient Descent always reaches a global minimum.
The supplied material establishes a particular convergence rate.

required_points: The step size determines the time increment used by each Forward Euler step.
forbidden_or_unsupported_points: A smaller step size always makes Forward Euler stable.
The supplied material establishes an optimal step size.

required_points: Maximum Likelihood Estimation uses the log-likelihood as the objective it maximizes.
forbidden_or_unsupported_points: Maximizing log-likelihood always produces an unbiased estimator.
The log-likelihood is itself the statistical model.

required_points: Newton's root-finding method can be applied to the score equation within Maximum Likelihood Estimation.
forbidden_or_unsupported_points: Maximum Likelihood Estimation always uses Newton's method.
Newton's method guarantees the global maximum of the likelihood.

required_points: Linear Regression fitting uses the least-squares objective by minimizing prediction residuals.
forbidden_or_unsupported_points: Least squares is the only possible objective for Linear Regression.
Minimizing least squares guarantees causal correctness.

required_points: The Newton Optimisation Method updates parameters in order to optimize an objective function.
forbidden_or_unsupported_points: Every objective function has an invertible Hessian.
Newton optimisation always reaches the global optimum.

required_points: The score function supplies the stationarity equation used to identify an interior Maximum Likelihood candidate.
forbidden_or_unsupported_points: Every zero of the score function is a global maximum.
The score equation alone guarantees estimator consistency.

required_points: The first-order Taylor formula requires the gradient as its linear term.
forbidden_or_unsupported_points: The gradient alone determines every higher-order term.
The formula is exact without additional conditions.

required_points: The Gradient Descent update requires the step size to scale the gradient term.
forbidden_or_unsupported_points: Any positive step size guarantees descent.
The step size determines the gradient itself.

required_points: The Forward Euler update requires the step size as its time increment.
forbidden_or_unsupported_points: The step size guarantees numerical stability.
Forward Euler chooses the step size automatically.

required_points: Maximum Likelihood Estimation requires a statistical model because the likelihood is defined from that model.
forbidden_or_unsupported_points: The statistical model is learned only after the likelihood is maximized.
Any statistical model guarantees an identifiable maximum-likelihood estimate.

required_points: The Gradient Descent update requires the gradient term that determines the update direction.
forbidden_or_unsupported_points: The update defines the gradient.
Having a gradient guarantees convergence.

required_points: The Newton Optimisation update requires the Hessian matrix through its inverse-curvature term.
forbidden_or_unsupported_points: Every Hessian is invertible.
Using the Hessian guarantees a better update than Gradient Descent.

required_points: The first-order Taylor formula is the formal mathematical expression of the first-order Taylor approximation.
forbidden_or_unsupported_points: Any formula containing a gradient formalizes Taylor approximation.
The formalization supplies an exact equality in every context.

required_points: The Gradient Descent update formula specifies one iteration of the Gradient Descent method.
forbidden_or_unsupported_points: The method formalizes the formula.
The displayed update proves convergence.

required_points: The mean-squared-error formula expresses the least-squares objective up to positive scaling.
forbidden_or_unsupported_points: Mean squared error and least squares are numerically identical under every scaling convention.
The formula guarantees a unique fitted model.

required_points: The Newton root update formula specifies one iteration of Newton's root-finding method.
forbidden_or_unsupported_points: The method is guaranteed to converge from every starting point.
The method formalizes the update formula rather than the formula formalizing the method.

required_points: Ridge Regression extends Ordinary Least Squares by adding a coefficient penalty.
forbidden_or_unsupported_points: Ridge Regression always improves predictive accuracy.
Ordinary Least Squares is a special case only when every coefficient is zero.

required_points: The Damped Newton Method extends Newton's root-finding method by scaling its correction.
forbidden_or_unsupported_points: Damping guarantees convergence.
Newton's original method extends the damped method.

required_points: Gradient Descent uses a step-size-controlled gradient move, whereas Newton optimisation adjusts its move using curvature information.
forbidden_or_unsupported_points: Newton optimisation is always faster or better than Gradient Descent.
The two methods cannot be used on the same objective.
